channel.title: CSOH - Cloud Security News
channel.description: Latest cloud security news curated by Cloud Security Office Hours. Covers AWS, Azure, GCP, Kubernetes vulnerabilities, breaches, and more.

item.title: ISC Stormcast For Thursday, April 23rd, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9904, (Thu, Apr 23rd)
LABScon25 Replay | Are Your Chinese Cameras Spying For You Or On You?
item.description: Marc Rogers and Silas Cutler expose how cheap smart home devices conceal a shadow supply chain of shell companies, firmware flaws, and foreign data routing.

item.title: Apple fixes iOS bug that retained deleted notification data
item.description: Apple has released out-of-band security updates for iPhone and iPad devices to fix a Notification Services flaw that could allow notifications marked for deletion to remain stored on the device. [...]

item.title: 'The Gentlemen' Rapidly Rises to Ransomware Prominence
item.description: Not nearly as polite as the name suggests, the ransomware gang has impressed researchers with its speed in scaling up operations — and its sophistication.

item.title: New Mirai campaign exploits RCE flaw in EoL D-Link routers
item.description: A new Mirai-based malware campaign is actively exploiting CVE-2025-29635, a high-severity command-injection vulnerability affecting D-Link DIR-823X routers, to enlist devices into the botnet. [...]

item.title: Google’s Workspace Intelligence promises privacy while running on your data
item.description: Security and data governance are among the key considerations in Google’s latest AI update, which introduces Workspace Intelligence within Google Workspace. Google describes the feature as “a secure, dynamic system th...

item.title: CISA Adds One Known Exploited Vulnerability to Catalog
item.description: CISA has added one new vulnerability to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2026-33825 Microsoft Defender Insufficient Granularity of Access Control Vulner...

item.title: Kyber ransomware gang toys with post-quantum encryption on Windows
item.description: A new Kyber ransomware operation is targeting Windows systems and VMware ESXi endpoints in recent attacks, with one variant implementing Kyber1024 post-quantum encryption. [...]

item.title: DDoS wave continues as Mastodon hit after Bluesky incident
item.description: Mastodon suffered a major DDoS attack shortly after a similar incident hit Bluesky. The outage was significant but resolved within a few hours. Mastodon was hit by a major DDoS attack just days after a similar disrupt...

item.title: Malicious KICS Docker Images and VS Code Extensions Hit Checkmarx Supply Chain
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have warned of malicious images pushed to the official "checkmarx/kics" Docker Hub repository. In an alert published today, software supply chain security company Socket revealed that unknown...

item.title: Self-Propagating Supply Chain Worm Hijacks npm Packages to Steal Developer Tokens
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a fresh set of packages that have been compromised by bad actors to deliver a self-propagating worm that spreads through stolen developer npm tokens. The supply chain worm has be...

item.title: Mirai Botnet exploits CVE-2025-29635 to target legacy D-Link routers
item.description: Mirai botnet is targeting old D-Link routers using CVE-2025-29635, a command injection flaw exploitable via crafted POST requests after public PoC disclosure. A Mirai botnet is actively exploiting a command injection...

item.title: Cyberattack on French government agency triggers phishing alert
item.description: France Titres, a French government agency, has disclosed a data breach that may have exposed user data from its online portal. France Titres, also known as the Agence nationale des titres sécurisés (ANTS), operates un...

item.title: A technical walkthrough of multicloud full-stack security using AWS Security Hub Extended
item.description: Building on our recent announcement of AWS Security Hub Extended —our full-stack enterprise security offering — we want to show you how we’re simplifying security procurement and operations for your multicloud environ...

item.title: Harvester Deploys Linux GoGra Backdoor in South Asia Using Microsoft Graph API
item.description: The threat actor known as Harvester has been attributed to a new Linux version of its GoGra backdoor deployed as part of attacks likely targeting entities in South Asia. "The malware uses the legitimate Microsoft Grap...

item.title: NCSC Unveils SilentGlass, a Plug-In Device to Protect Monitors from Cyber-Attacks
item.description: The UK’s cybersecurity agency said the devices will be available for purchase by organizations around the world

item.title: DPRK Fake Job Scams Self-Propagate in 'Contagious Interview'
item.description: A compromised developer's repository serves as a worm-like infection vector to spread remote access Trojans (RATs) and other malware.

item.title: Claude Mythos finds 271 Firefox flaws, Mozilla believes it shifts security toward defenders
item.description: The Mozilla Foundation tested Claude Mythos, an Anthropic AI model that has stirred debate in the cybersecurity community. Before granting access to Mythos, Mozilla scanned Firefox using Opus 4.6, which led to fixes f...

item.title: After Bluesky, Mastodon Targeted in DDoS Attack
item.description: The DDoS attack caused a major outage, but Mastodon mitigated it within a few hours. The post After Bluesky, Mastodon Targeted in DDoS Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: UK Commits £90m for Cybersecurity and Pushes for ‘Resilience Pledge’
item.description: UK unveils £90m cybersecurity funding at CYBERUK to boost SME resilience, promote Cyber Essentials and a new Cyber Resilience Pledge, sparking industry debate

item.title: Inside Caller-as-a-Service Fraud: The Scam Economy Has a Hiring Process
item.description: Fraud operations now operate like call centers, complete with hiring, training, and performance tracking. Flare reveals how cybercriminals manage "Caller-as-a-Service" operations like a professional sales team. [...]

item.title: Microsoft out-of-band updates fixed critical ASP.NET Core privilege escalation flaw
item.description: Microsoft fixed critical ASP.NET Core vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-40372 (CVSS score of 9.1), that lets attackers escalate privileges. Microsoft released out-of-band updates to address a serious ASP.NET Core vul...

item.title: SnowFROC 2026: Secure Defaults, Real Trust, and a Better Layer on Top
item.description: This year's Devner OWASP event showed why modern AppSec depends on secure defaults, stronger provenance, and security controls that appear where developers make decisions.

item.title: Prove Identity Platform connects verification, authentication, and fraud prevention
item.description: Prove has launched the Prove Identity Platform, turning identity verification into an ongoing, real-time process for users, businesses, and AI agents. AI agents are already initiating real transactions on behalf of re...

item.title: Critical BRIDGE:BREAK flaws impact Lantronix and Silex Technology converters
item.description: 22 BRIDGE:BREAK flaws hit Lantronix and Silex Technology converters, exposing approximately 20,000 devices to hijacking and data tampering. Researchers at Forescout Research Vedere Labs found 22 BRIDGE:BREAK flaws in...

item.title: New Mirai variants target routers and DVRs in parallel campaigns
item.description: Hidden inside newly discovered botnet malware is an unusual message from its creator: “AI.NEEDS.TO.DIE”. Dubbed “tuxnokill” by researchers at Akamai, the malware is one of two fresh Mirai botnet variants documented th...

item.title: Acronis GenAI Protection gives MSPs control over AI usage and data risks
item.description: Acronis has launced Acronis GenAI Protection, a monitoring and security solution that enables managed service providers (MSPs) to control generative AI usage across client environments, preventing sensitive data expos...

item.title: Surge in Silent Subject Phishing Attacks Targets VIP Users
item.description: Null subject phishing campaigns bypass filters and target VIPs with QR code and RMM abuse

item.title: New npm supply-chain attack self-spreads to steal auth tokens
item.description: A new supply chain attack targeting the Node Package Manager (npm) ecosystem is stealing developer credentials and attempting to spread through packages published from compromised accounts. [...]

item.title: Most Serious Cyberattacks Against the UK Now From Russia, Iran and China, Cyber Chief Says
item.description: British businesses need to prepare themselves to defend against cyberattacks because the U.K. could be targeted “at scale,” if it became involved in an international conflict. The post Most Serious Cyberattacks Agains...

item.title: Elastic MCP Apps bring security and observability workflows into AI tools
item.description: Elastic has announced MCP Apps for Elastic, delivering agent-native UI experiences for security and observability workflows across third-party coding tools and chat clients. The new MCP Apps enable teams to investigat...

item.title: New Wiper Malware Targeted Venezuelan Energy Sector Prior to US Intervention
item.description: Dubbed Lotus Wiper, the malware targets recovery mechanisms, overwrites drives, and systematically deletes files. The post New Wiper Malware Targeted Venezuelan Energy Sector Prior to US Intervention appeared first on...

item.title: Wiz at Google Next: Machine-Speed Defense for Any Cloud, Any Platform, Any AI
item.description: Announcing new capabilities at Google Cloud Next that extend and deepen Wiz AI-APP coverage: From the first line of AI-generated code, across AI and agent studios, to the edge of the cloud

item.title: Mirai Botnet Targets Flaw in Discontinued D-Link Routers
item.description: The exploitation of the command injection vulnerability started one year after public disclosure and PoC exploit code publication. The post Mirai Botnet Targets Flaw in Discontinued D-Link Routers appeared first on Se...

item.title: Are SBOMs Failing? Supply Chain Attacks Rise as Security Teams Struggle With SBOM Data
item.description: Researcher says the missing piece is a governance-driven intelligence layer that turns SBOM and VEX data into explainable security decisions. The post Are SBOMs Failing? Supply Chain Attacks Rise as Security Teams Str...

item.title: Progress Software fixes sneaky WAF bypass vulnerability (CVE-2026-21876)
item.description: Progress Software has fixed a slew of high-severity vulnerabilities in MOVEit WAF and LoadMaster, including a flaw (CVE-2026-21876) that may allow attackers to bypass firewall detection. MOVEit WAF (web application fi...

item.title: Claude Mythos Finds 271 Firefox Vulnerabilities
item.description: All the flaws could have also been found by an elite human researcher, according to Mozilla. The post Claude Mythos Finds 271 Firefox Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Former Ransomware Negotiator Pleads Guilty to Working For BlackCat Cyber Gang
item.description: A former ransomware negotiator has pleaded guilty to abusing his position by working with noted cybercrime group BlackCat

item.title: Lotus Wiper Malware Targets Venezuelan Energy Systems in Destructive Attack
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a previously undocumented data wiper that has been used in attacks targeting Venezuela at the end of last year and the start of 2026. Dubbed Lotus Wiper, the novel file wiper...

item.title: North Korean Hackers Use AppleScript, ClickFix in Fresh macOS Attacks
item.description: The campaigns focus on financial organizations, including cryptocurrency, venture capital, and blockchain entities. The post North Korean Hackers Use AppleScript, ClickFix in Fresh macOS Attacks appeared first on Secu...

item.title: Toxic Combinations: When Cross-App Permissions Stack into Risk
item.description: On January 31, 2026, researchers disclosed that Moltbook, a social network built for AI agents, had left its database wide open, exposing 35,000 email addresses and 1.5 million agent API tokens across 770,000 active a...

item.title: Tencent’s QClaw AI agent app arrives on Windows and macOS
item.description: Tencent has opened an international beta of QClaw, an AI agent application aimed at consumers in Canada, Japan, Singapore, South Korea, and the United States. The first wave is capped at 20,000 users. Additional marke...

item.title: IR Trends Q1 2026: Phishing reemerges as top initial access vector, as attacks targeting public administration persist
item.description: Phishing reemerged as the most observed means of gaining initial access, accounting for over a third of the engagements where initial access could be determined. Phishing has not been the top vertical for initial acce...

item.title: When Wi-Fi Encryption Fails: Protecting Your Enterprise from AirSnitch Attacks
item.description: Unit 42 research reveals AirSnitch attacks bypass WPA2/3 Wi-Fi encryption and client isolation, exposing critical infrastructure vulnerabilities. The post When Wi-Fi Encryption Fails: Protecting Your Enterprise from A...

item.title: Phishing reclaims the top initial access spot, attackers experiment with AI tools
item.description: Phishing returned as the leading method attackers used to break into organizations in the first quarter of 2026, accounting for over a third of engagements where initial access could be determined, according to Cisco...

item.title: New GoGra malware for Linux uses Microsoft Graph API for comms
item.description: A Linux variant of the GoGra backdoor uses legitimate Microsoft infrastructure, relying on an Outlook inbox for stealthy payload delivery. [...]

item.title: Researchers Uncover ProxySmart Software Powering 90+ SIM Farms
item.description: Infrawatch says ProxySmart platform enables SIM farm activity at “industrial scale”

item.title: Google Antigravity in Crosshairs of Security Researchers, Cybercriminals
item.description: Researchers discovered a remote code execution vulnerability and cybercriminals are using its reputation to deliver malware. The post Google Antigravity in Crosshairs of Security Researchers, Cybercriminals appeared f...

item.title: OneDrive updates focus on AI, access control, and compliance
item.description: Microsoft OneDrive’s recent updates focus on improving intelligence, collaboration, and administrative control. “Last year, we made a promise: your files should work for you, not the other way around. That meant reima...

item.title: Microsoft Patches Critical ASP.NET Core CVE-2026-40372 Privilege Escalation Bug
item.description: Microsoft has released out-of-band updates to address a security vulnerability in ASP.NET Core that could allow an attacker to escalate privileges. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-40372, carries a CVSS score of...

item.title: Oracle Patches 450 Vulnerabilities With April 2026 CPU
item.description: The company released 481 new security patches across 28 product families, including over 300 fixes for remotely exploitable, unauthenticated flaws. The post Oracle Patches 450 Vulnerabilities With April 2026 CPU appea...

item.title: Microsoft releases emergency patches for critical ASP.NET flaw
item.description: Microsoft has released out-of-band (OOB) security updates to patch a critical ASP.NET Core privilege escalation vulnerability. [...]

item.title: UK Faces a Cyber ‘Perfect Storm’ Driven by Tech Advances and Nation State Threats, NCSC Warns
item.description: The convergence of global tensions and rapid technological change is driving a new era of cyber risk, the NCSC warns

item.title: Mustang Panda’s New LOTUSLITE Variant Targets India Banks, South Korea Policy Circles
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a new variant of a known malware called LOTUSLITE that's distributed via a theme related to India's banking sector. "The backdoor communicates with a dynamic DNS-based command...

item.title: Venezuela energy sector targeted by highly destructive Lotus wiper
item.description: Lotus Wiper hit Venezuelan energy systems, used scripts to disable defenses, then erased all data beyond recovery. Kaspersky researchers found Lotus Wiper targeting Venezuela’s energy and utilities sector amid regiona...

item.title: Cohere AI Terrarium Sandbox Flaw Enables Root Code Execution, Container Escape
item.description: A critical security vulnerability has been disclosed in a Python-based sandbox called Terrarium that could result in arbitrary code execution. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-5752, is rated 9.3 on the CVSS scor...

item.title: Over 1,300 Microsoft SharePoint servers vulnerable to spoofing attacks
item.description: Over 1,300 Microsoft SharePoint servers exposed online remain unpatched against a spoofing vulnerability that was exploited as a zero-day and is still being abused in ongoing attacks. [...]

item.title: PentAGI: Open-source autonomous AI penetration testing system
item.description: Penetration testers have long relied on collections of specialized tools, manual coordination, and documented runbooks to work through a target assessment. PentAGI, an open-source project from VXControl, attempts to a...

item.title: Apple Intelligence flaw kept stolen tokens reusable on another device
item.description: Apple claims that Apple Intelligence, a GenAI service provided on its operating systems, is designed with an extra focus on user security and privacy through a two-stage authentication and authorization system using a...

item.title: Shadow AI, deepfakes, and supply chain compromise are rewriting the financial sector threat playbook
item.description: Financially motivated attacks continued to drive the bulk of cyber incidents against banks, insurers, and payment processors in 2025. Approximately 90% of breaches affecting financial institutions carried a financial...

item.title: ISC Stormcast For Wednesday, April 22nd, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9902, (Wed, Apr 22nd)
Winter 2025 SOC 1 report is now available with 184 services in scope
item.description: Amazon Web Services (AWS) is pleased to announce that the Winter 2025 System and Organization Controls (SOC) 1 report is now available. The report covers 184 services over the 12-month period from January 1, 2025 – De...

item.title: [Guest Diary] Beyond Cryptojacking: Telegram tdata as a Credential Harvesting Vector, Lessons from a Honeypot Incident, (Wed, Apr 22nd)
item.description: &#;x26;#;x5b;This is a Guest Diary by L. Carty, an ISC intern as part of the SANS.edu Bachelor&#;x26;#;39;s Degree in Applied Cybersecurity (BACS) program &#;x26;#;x5b;1].]

item.title: CVE-2026-33626: How attackers exploited LMDeploy LLM Inference Engines in 12 hours
item.description: CVE-2026-33626 in LMDeploy was exploited within 12 hours of disclosure, enabling attackers to use a vision-LLM endpoint for SSRF-based internal network scanning, cloud metadata access, and service enumeration.

item.title: Chainguard customers safe from new npm worm and xinference supply chain attack
item.description: New npm and PyPI malware hit many popular packages. Chainguard customers stayed protected by blocking install scripts and rebuilding only verified source code.

item.title: U.S. CISA adds Cisco Catalyst, Kentico Xperience, PaperCut NG/MF, Synacor ZCS, Quest KACE SMA, and JetBrains TeamCity flaws to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog
item.description: U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) adds Cisco Catalyst, Kentico Xperience, PaperCut NG/MF, Synacor ZCS, Quest KACE SMA, and JetBrains TeamCity flaws to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities cat...

item.title: Vercel Confirms Cyber Incident After Sophisticated Attacker Exploits Third‑Party Tool
item.description: Cloud app developer Vercel appears to have suffered a security breach

item.title: A .WAV With A Payload, (Tue, Apr 21st)
item.description: There have been reports of threat actors using a .wav file as a vector for malware.

item.title: CISA Adds 8 Exploited Flaws to KEV, Sets April-May 2026 Federal Deadlines
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Monday added eight new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, including three flaws impacting Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Ma...

item.title: Apache ActiveMQ RCE
item.description: What is the Vulnerability? CVE-2026-34197 is a high-severity remote code execution (RCE) vulnerability affecting Apache ActiveMQ Classic. The flaw resides in the exposed Jolokia JMX-HTTP interface and allows attackers...

Thunderbird 150 arrives with encrypted message search and OpenPGP improvements
item.description: Released today, Thunderbird 150.0 brings eight new features, a round of bug fixes, and security patches that cover the web engine underlying the email client. Thunderbird 150.0 runs on Windows 10 or later, macOS 10.15...

Introducing the CrowdStrike Shadow AI Visibility Service
Kyber Ransomware Double Trouble: Windows and ESXi Attacks Explained
item.description: Overview For executive leadership, the emergence of Kyber ransomware represents a significant and immediate threat due to its specialized, dual-platform deployment capability targeting mission-critical virtualization...

item.title: From Bulk Export to AI-ready Security Workflows: Introducing Rapid7’s Open-Source MCP Server and Agent Skill
item.description: Security teams want more from their data than APIs and one-off reports. They want to ask better questions, move faster, and bring security context into the workflows they are already building. That’s especially true a...

item.title: Introducing the CrowdStrike Shadow AI Visibility Service
New Cloud Security Alliance Survey Reveals 82% of Enterprises Have Unknown AI Agents in Their Environments
item.description: Lack of visibility remains a top security risk with 65% of respondents reporting AI agent-related incidents in the past year SEATTLE – April 21, 2026 – A new survey report from the Cloud Security Alliance (CSA), the w...

item.title: French govt agency confirms breach as hacker offers to sell data
item.description: France Titres, the government agency in France for issuing and managince administrative documents has disclosed a data breach after a threat actor claimed the attack and stealing citizen data. [...]

Ransomware Negotiator Pleads Guilty to BlackCat Scheme
item.description: A cautionary tale illustrates why the person negotiating should never be involved with any part of the ransom payment process, experts noted.

item.title: Ransomware negotiator caught secretly assisting BlackCat extortion scheme
item.description: Angelo Martino pleaded guilty to helping BlackCat ransomware group while acting as a ransomware negotiator. Another U.S. cybersecurity expert, Angelo Martino, admitted helping the BlackCat ransomware group while worki...

item.title: Exploits Turn Windows Defender into Attacker Tool
item.description: Three proof-of-concept exploits are being used in active attacks against Microsoft's built-in security platform; two are unpatched.

item.title: SystemBC C2 Server Reveals 1,570+ Victims in The Gentlemen Ransomware Operation
item.description: Threat actors associated with The Gentlemen ransomware‑as‑a‑service (RaaS) operation have been observed attempting to deploy a known proxy malware called SystemBC. According to new research published by Check Point, t...

New Lotus data wiper used against Venezuelan energy, utility firms
item.description: A previously undocumented data-wiping malware dubbed Lotus was used last year in targeted attacks against energy and utilities organizations in Venezuela. [...]

item.title: VirtualBox 7.2.8 is out with Linux kernel 7.0 support and crash fixes
item.description: Oracle shipped VirtualBox 7.2.8 on April 21, 2026, as a maintenance release covering crashes, networking problems, clipboard issues, and extended Linux kernel compatibility. The update touches the VMM layer, NAT netwo...

item.title: Surge in Bomgar RMM Exploitation Demonstrates Supply Chain Risk
item.description: The critical remote code execution flaw (CVE-2026-1731) in the remote monitoring and management tool can be exploited to spread ransomware and compromise supply chains.

item.title: Introducing the CrowdStrike Shadow AI Visibility Service
Trojanized Android App Fuels New Wave of NFC Fraud
item.description: NGate malware abuses HandyPay app to steal NFC card data and PINs in Brazil

item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have identified 22 new vulnerabilities in popular models of serial-to-IP converters from Lantronix and Silex that could be exploited to hijack susceptible devices and tamper with data exchang...

item.title: Ransomware negotiator admits role in attacks he was hired to resolve
item.description: A Florida man, formerly employed as a ransomware negotiator, pleaded guilty to conspiring to carry out ransomware attacks against US companies. Prosecutors say Angelo Martino, 41, used his position at DigitalMint, a c...

item.title: Google Fixes Critical RCE Flaw in AI-Based Antigravity Tool
item.description: The prompt injection vulnerability in the agentic AI product for filesystem operations was a sanitization issue that allowed for sandbox escape and arbitrary code execution.

item.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
item.description: A 24-year-old British national and senior member of the cybercrime group "Scattered Spider" has pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Tyler Robert Buchanan admitted his role in a serie...

item.title: Third US Security Expert Admits Helping Ransomware Gang
item.description: Angelo Martino of Florida has pleaded guilty to collaborating with the BlackCat cybercrime group while working as a ransomware negotiator. The post Third US Security Expert Admits Helping Ransomware Gang appeared firs...

item.title: Ransomware Negotiator Pleads Guilty to Aiding BlackCat Attacks in 2023
item.description: A third individual who was employed as a ransomware negotiator has pleaded guilty to conducting ransomware attacks against U.S. companies in 2023. Angelo Martino, 41, of Land O'Lakes, Florida, teamed up with the opera...

item.title: Dozens of Malicious Crypto Apps Land in Apple App Store
item.description: Masquerading as popular cryptocurrency wallets, the apps can hijack recovery phrases and private keys. The post Dozens of Malicious Crypto Apps Land in Apple App Store appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Stopping Fraud at Each Stage of the Customer Journey Without Adding Friction
item.description: Fraud prevention and user experience don't have to be a tradeoff. IPQS shows how combining identity, device, and network signals stops fraud without adding friction. [...]

item.title: The Gentlemen Ransomware Expands With Rapid Affiliate Growth
item.description: Gentlemen RaaS expands quickly with multi-platform attacks and SystemBC-linked infections

item.title: 5 Places where Mature SOCs Keep MTTR Fast and Others Waste Time
item.description: Security teams often present MTTR as an internal KPI. Leadership sees it differently: every hour a threat dwells inside the environment is an hour of potential data exfiltration, service disruption, regulatory exposur...

item.title: Closing the Security Gap in the Age of Agentic Coding
item.description: AI agents are writing more and more code. Ensure it's secure. Learn how Wiz Code plugins and the Green Agent bring machine-speed remediation to your AI-IDE.

item.description: A British national tied to the Scattered Spider cybercrime group pleaded guilty to hacking multiple companies via SMS phishing and stealing over $8 million in virtual currency from US victims. Tyler Robert Buchanan, 2...

item.title: Moving past bots vs. humans
item.description: As AI assistants and privacy proxies challenge the capabilities of traditional bot detection, the Web needs new models for accountability. We believe that control should remain with the client, and that an open ecosys...

item.title: Unchecked AI Agents Cause Cybersecurity Incidents at Two Thirds of Firms
item.description: Data exposure, operational disruption and financial losses among issues faced by businesses struggling with the rapid rise of AI agents, warns Cloud Security Alliance report

item.description: Silobreaker has announced new agentic AI capabilities that combine faster research and deeper contextual analysis with built-in governance and transparency to ensure trusted intelligence can be safely consumed across...

item.title: Unsecured Perforce Servers Expose Sensitive Data From Major Orgs
item.description: Things are improving, but a researcher has still identified over 1,500 Perforce P4 instances allowing attackers to read files on the server. The post Unsecured Perforce Servers Expose Sensitive Data From Major Orgs ap...

item.title: CISA flags new SD-WAN flaw as actively exploited in attacks
item.description: ​CISA has given U.S. government agencies four days to secure their systems against another Catalyst SD-WAN Manager vulnerability it flagged as actively exploited in attacks. [...]

item.title: [Podcast] It's not you, it's your printer: State-sponsored and phishing threats in 2025
item.description: In this episode of Talos Takes, Amy and Martin Lee unpack state-sponsored and phishing trends from the 2025 Talos Year in Review.

item.title: OpenAI’s Chronicle feature lets Codex read your screen, raising privacy concerns
item.description: OpenAI’s Chronicle is a feature designed to help Codex, an AI-powered coding assistant, better understand what users are working on by capturing context directly from their screens. It uses recent screen activity to b...

item.title: Progress Patches Multiple Vulnerabilities in MOVEit WAF, LoadMaster
item.description: The security defects could be exploited for remote code execution, OS command injection, and WAF detection bypass. The post Progress Patches Multiple Vulnerabilities in MOVEit WAF, LoadMaster appeared first on Securit...

item.title: Phishing and MFA exploitation: Targeting the keys to the kingdom
item.description: In 2025, attackers increasingly targeted weaknesses in multi-factor authentication (MFA) workflows, and phishing attacks leveraged valid, compromised credentials to launch lures from trusted accounts. The trends focus...

item.title: Mapping Your API Ecosystem: Wiz Expands API Discovery with Apigee
item.description: See your full Apigee architecture on the Wiz Security Graph, from API gateways and environments to every endpoint and its authorization scheme.

item.title: No Exploit Needed: How Attackers Walk Through the Front Door via Identity-Based Attacks
item.description: The cybersecurity industry has spent the last several years chasing sophisticated threats like zero-days, supply chain compromises, and AI-generated exploits. However, the most reliable entry point for attackers still...

item.title: CISA flags another Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager bug as exploited (CVE-2026-20133)
item.description: CISA added eight new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, including a Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager vulnerability (CVE-2026-20133) that Cisco has yet to flag as exploited. Three Cisco...

item.title: Organizations Warned of Exploited Cisco, Kentico, Zimbra Vulnerabilities
item.description: CISA expanded the KEV catalog with eight flaws, but five of them have been flagged as exploited before. The post Organizations Warned of Exploited Cisco, Kentico, Zimbra Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Actively exploited Apache ActiveMQ flaw impacts 6,400 servers
item.description: Nonprofit security organization Shadowserver found that over 6,400 Apache ActiveMQ servers exposed online are vulnerable to ongoing attacks exploiting a high-severity code injection vulnerability. [...]

item.description: Data breaches were disclosed by Southern Illinois Dermatology, Saint Anthony Hospital, and North Texas Behavioral Health Authority. The post Data Breaches at Healthcare Organizations in Illinois and Texas Affect 600,0...

item.title: NGate Campaign Targets Brazil, Trojanizes HandyPay to Steal NFC Data and PINs
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a new iteration of an Android malware family called NGate that has been found to abuse a legitimate application called HandyPay instead of NFCGate. "The threat actors took the...
